godot_contract_refs.section: Production BattleSetup, BattleLaunchRequest, BattleSetupBuilder, and BattleSetupValidator ownership
Fixture identity, setup-source compilation boundary, and deterministic coverage declarations
P2 fixture schema/compiler ordering and P7 production setup ownership

source_evidence_refs.symbol: script_test::Parser::Parse, ParseSetupBlock, CheckSetupBlock, ParseExecutionBlock, and AddAssertion

known_ambiguities: The clean script container, parser, and tester are structural evidence for data-driven setup, inputs, checkpoints, validation, repetition, and result aggregation only; their proprietary text syntax, values, identifiers, fixtures, and permissive parsing behavior are not copied or treated as Godot contract truth.

P2 Todo 6 remains open: chapter 19 requires CANONICAL_SETUP to use the production BattleSetupValidator and LAUNCH_REQUEST to use the production BattleSetupBuilder plus the same validator, while chapter 22 assigns those production contracts to P7.

This work item emits only a deterministic requirement manifest derived from already validated SCENARIO TestManifestEntry records; it does not define or accept setup_source, publish an executable BattleFixture, create a fixture-only setup DTO, or calculate an expected setup digest.

Any synthetic scenario fixture file fails with P2D_SETUP_COMPILER_UNAVAILABLE_P7 until P7 supplies the exact production setup schemas, builder, validator, catalog/profile/hash checks, default expansion, and source ports.
Declared requirements never count as executed, observed, passed, or coverage-promoting evidence, and the requirement manifest is not added to the runtime rule catalog or its hash.
The tracked mechanism, event, handler, resolver, test, and scenario fixture sets remain empty, so no source payload, proprietary value, or generated fixture artifact enters Git.
